svg.title: LLM-Native Multi-Agent CSO Architecture
svg.desc: Two LLM agents — Auth Score Agent powered by Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Cost Agent powered by GPT-5.4 — fan out from an orchestrator, return structured JSON scores, which are combined and filtered through a compliance gate before producing the final scheme decision.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What transaction data is passed into the LLM agents?')
g.text: BIN · card type · merchant · MCC · amount · channel · 3DS

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the LLM orchestrator do in the CSO multi-agent design?')
g.text: Task decomposition · parallel agent dispatch · result aggregation

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the Auth Score Agent use Claude Sonnet 4.6?')

g.text: issuer auth rate history · decline code taxonomy

svg.text: Why Sonnet 4.6: structured reasoning · fast tool-use loops
safe outputs · 200K context for issuer history

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the Cost Agent use GPT-5.4?')

g.text: MCC tier rules · merchant volume bands

svg.text: Why GPT-5.4: numerical precision · agentic tool loops
87% FinQA benchmark · fast structured JSON output

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How are the auth score and cost score combined into a final ranking?')
g.text: scheme* = argmax [ w₁ × p_auth − w₂ × norm(fee) ] w₁ ≫ w₂

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What compliance rules does the gate enforce on the LLM scheme recommendation?')
g.text: EU IFR · Durbin · Amex OptBlue · token lock · merchant eligibility

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the final scheme decision response look like?')

svg.text: Orchestration + aggregation — deep reasoning, task planning
Auth scoring — structured tool-use, safe outputs, payments reasoning
